content.plain_english: This week in Year 2 maths, your child is working on Consolidation. The learning focuses on: solve problems with addition and subtraction:; using concrete objects and pictorial representations, including those involving numbers, quantities and measures; and applying increasing knowledge of mental and written methods. These skills build directly on what they have already mastered and prepare them for the next steps in the programme of study.
content.in_school: In lessons, children use practical resources and visual representations to explore consolidation. They work from hands-on activities through to diagrams and then written methods, with class discussion and partner work helping every child understand and apply what they have learned.
content.home_activity: Spend 5 minutes exploring consolidation together. Ask your child to explain what they have been learning in school — teaching someone else is one of the best ways to understand something deeply. Use everyday objects if that helps, and follow your child's lead.
dinner_table_questions: Can you show me how to solve problems with addition and subtraction:?
Was it easy or tricky to using concrete objects and pictorial representations, including those involving numbers, quantities and measures? Why?
How would you explain today's maths to someone who has never seen it before?

key_vocabulary.definition: Look back over what you have learned to check and strengthen understanding.
Use your knowledge in a new or different situation.
Explain why an answer is correct using mathematical language.
Working through a challenge by choosing and using the right maths skills.
A planned method used to approach and solve a problem.

example_questions: Write three hundred and twelve in numerals.
content.times_table_tip: Year 2 children learn their 2, 5, 10, and 3 times tables. The 3 times table is the new one this year. Try chanting together: 3, 6, 9, 12, 15 … Clapping a beat as you say the numbers helps the rhythm stick.
